blocks.text: The path to prayer begins with the most basic acts of personal hygiene. Istinja is the ritual cleaning of the private areas after relieving oneself. While it may seem like a simple matter of hygiene, in Islam, it is a sacred duty that ensures we are physically and ritually prepared to stand before Allah. Maintaining this level of purity is a hallmark of the believer's commitment to excellence in every aspect of life.

Istinja is obligatory whenever any impurity (Najasah) is discharged from the body. It is a prerequisite for the validity of Wudu and, consequently, the validity of the prayer itself. The Prophet ﷺ warned that neglecting to clean oneself properly after urination is one of the primary causes of punishment in the grave, underscoring the gravity of this seemingly small act.

Istinja is typically performed with water, which is the most effective and preferred method. However, Islam also provides the alternative of 'Istijmar', which is cleaning with solid, pure materials like stones or tissues. To be valid, Istijmar must use at least three stones (or three wipes) and must completely remove the impurity. The goal is 'Inqa'—complete cleanliness—so that no trace of the impurity remains.

blocks.textEn: Narrated Anas:Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) went to answer the call of nature, he used to say, "Allah-umma inni a`udhu bika minal khubuthi wal khaba'ith i.e. O Allah, I seek Refuge with You from all offensive and wicked things (evil deeds and evil spirits)

blocks.text: This hadith provides practical details on how the Prophet ﷺ and his companions maintained purity. It shows that the Prophet ﷺ would use water for cleaning himself after relieving himself. The attention to these details highlights that nothing in a believer's life is too small for divine guidance, and that physical purity is the foundation upon which the spiritual edifice of prayer is built.

A common mistake is rushing this process or assuming that a cursory wipe is sufficient. Scholars emphasize that one must be certain that the impurity has been completely removed. If using water, ensure the area is cleaned thoroughly; if using stones or tissue, ensure at least three wipes are used and the area is left clean.

Approach Istinja with the intention of fulfilling a Sunnah and preparing for prayer. Use your left hand for cleaning, as taught by the Prophet ﷺ, and ensure you are in a private, screened area. This mindfulness even in the bathroom builds a habit of 'Taqwa' (God-consciousness) that carries over into your worship.

When you are confident in your physical purity, your mind is freed from the 'waswasa' (whispers) of doubt that can plague a person during prayer. By mastering these basics, you secure the foundation of your Khushu, knowing that you have met the requirements of purity as prescribed by Allah and His Messenger.
